legal_definition.text: Parkinson’s disease means a progressive neurological disorder of the central nervous system characterized by tremors, rigidity, slowness of movement and impaired balance and coordination.
legal_definition.statutory_source: Schedule to the Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

statutory_status.act: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act, 2016

benchmark_requirement.definition: Person with benchmark disability means a person with not less than forty per cent of a specified disability as certified by the certifying authority.

reservation_category.grouping_note: Not expressly named in Section 34(1) clauses; reservation applicability depends on identification of posts and government notifications.
reservation_category.eligibility_condition: Applicable only to persons with benchmark disability.

certification_workflow.assessment_guidelines: Neurological evaluation as per guidelines notified under Section 56.

legal_rights_triggered.assistive_support: Right to assistive devices and workplace adjustments

distinction_framework: Parkinson’s Disease is a progressive degenerative neurological condition.
It is distinct from Multiple Sclerosis and other neurological disabilities though functional effects may overlap.
Reservation grouping does not merge statutory classification.
legal_boundary_conditions: Reservation applies only to Government establishments as defined under the Act.
Benchmark threshold of 40% mandatory for reservation eligibility.
Medical assessment must follow officially notified neurological standards.
Certification valid throughout India unless lawfully revoked.
interpretive_notes: Symptoms may worsen progressively over time.
Functional impairment rather than diagnosis alone determines benchmark disability status.
Legal protections extend beyond reservation to education, employment and accessibility.

legal_schema.law_name: Rights of Persons with Disabilities Act
